laws.title: Public Law 27-192 | Presidential Libraries and Better Public Records Management Act
laws.status_basis: Full source recovered. The act contains a direct amendment to 18 U.S.C. 2071 and uncodified NARA/public-records directives. The current XML only preserves a note at Title 44 and does not apply the Title 18 amendment.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date, and definitions.
provisions.evidence: law.txt SECTION I defines the act and supplies severability/effective-date language.

provisions.text_summary: Amends Public Law 3-19 to add public-records duties and Trello-board ownership rules.
provisions.exact_change: Rewrite Sections II-IV of Public Law 3-19 to require timely NARA notice, Congressional and judicial record upkeep, and NARA ownership of the relevant Trello boards.
provisions.evidence: law.txt SECTION II expressly says Public Law 3-19 shall be amended and read with new Section II-IV text.
provisions.notes: This is an amendment to a prior public-law note, not to a Code section.

provisions.text_summary: Amends 18 U.S.C. 2071(a) and (b) to replace the penalty and operative language.
provisions.exact_change: Replace subsections (a) and (b) of 18 U.S.C. 2071 with the enacted ten-day and $10,000 language in SEC. III.
provisions.evidence: law.txt SECTION III expressly amends 18 U.S.C. 2071(a) and (b); current Title 18 text in usc18.xml still shows the unamended three-year penalty.
provisions.notes: This is the missing Code treatment.

provisions.text_summary: Requires executive-branch records to be created and maintained in public Trello boards.
provisions.evidence: law.txt SECTION IV imposes executive-records management duties without a numbered Code citation.

provisions.text_summary: Creates presidential-library setup and record-access obligations.
provisions.evidence: law.txt SECTION V directs NARA to help each President establish a Presidential Library and make records public via Trello.

current_implementation.detail: Current XML keeps only the Title 44 note; the enforceable 18 U.S.C. 2071 amendment is absent in both current XML and origin/main.

recommended_actions: apply the 18 U.S.C. 2071(a) and (b) amendment at Title 18
keep the Public Law 3-19 rewrite as a concise statutory-note amendment record
retain the executive-records and presidential-library directives as notes
remove the Trello link
laws.chronology_conclusion: Full source recovered. The act contains a direct amendment to 18 U.S.C. 2071 and uncodified NARA/public-records directives. The current XML only preserves a note at Title 44 and does not apply the Title 18 amendment.

current_xml_comparison.detail: Current XML keeps only the Title 44 note; the enforceable 18 U.S.C. 2071 amendment is absent in both current XML and origin/main.

source_evidence: Full source recovered. The act contains a direct amendment to 18 U.S.C. 2071 and uncodified NARA/public-records directives. The current XML only preserves a note at Title 44 and does not apply the Title 18 amendment.
laws.final_recommended_action: apply the 18 U.S.C. 2071(a) and (b) amendment at Title 18 | keep the Public Law 3-19 rewrite as a concise statutory-note amendment record | retain the executive-records and presidential-library directives as notes | remove the Trello link
laws.exact_change: Rewrite Sections II-IV of Public Law 3-19 to require timely NARA notice, Congressional and judicial record upkeep, and NARA ownership of the relevant Trello boards. | Replace subsections (a) and (b) of 18 U.S.C. 2071 with the enacted ten-day and $10,000 language in SEC. III.

laws.title: Public Law 27-193 | Preservation of Parks and Monuments Act
laws.status_basis: Full source recovered. This is a free-standing generally applicable parks and monuments statute with no express positive-law Code amendment, so it belongs as statutory history rather than permanent section text.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and immediate effective date.
provisions.evidence: law.txt sections 1-3 supply the title and general rule-of-construction provisions.

provisions.text_summary: Transfers park-system duties from the Interior Department and NPS to the Secretary of Homeland Security.
provisions.evidence: law.txt section 4 shifts authority over the parks system and DC park preservation functions.

provisions.text_summary: Authorizes regulations for parks and associated areas and concurrent jurisdiction around DC areas.
provisions.evidence: law.txt section 5 authorizes rules and regulations for park protection and public order.

provisions.text_summary: Transfers Park Police and Park Rangers responsibilities to the Federal Protective Service; source also contains a duplicate effective-date label.
provisions.evidence: law.txt section 6 moves Park Police and Park Ranger powers to FPS and repeats effective-date language.

current_implementation.detail: Current XML preserves the act as a statutory note at a Title 54 parks anchor.

recommended_actions: keep a concise statutory note
do not promote the act to permanent positive-law text
remove the Trello link

laws.title: Public Law 27-194 | Fast Food Workers Day Act of 2024
laws.status_basis: Full source recovered. The act makes a direct, determinable amendment to 5 U.S.C. 6103(a) by adding National Fast Food Workers Day. The current XML already contains the inserted holiday text.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: law.txt SECTION 1 identifies the act as the Fast Food Workers Day Act of 2024.

provisions.text_summary: Amends 5 U.S.C. 6103(a) to add National Fast Food Workers Day, June 7, after Memorial Day.
provisions.exact_change: Insert National Fast Food Workers Day, June 7, after the Memorial Day item in 5 U.S.C. 6103(a).
provisions.evidence: law.txt SECTION 2(a) directs the insertion; current usc05.xml already shows the new holiday text and amendment note rp-c8d95f71e8fe691d220e.
provisions.notes: Already integrated in current XML and origin/main.

provisions.text_summary: Severability.
provisions.evidence: law.txt SECTION 3 contains the severability clause.

current_implementation.detail: The 5 U.S.C. 6103(a) holiday text is already present in current XML; the note is history only.

recommended_actions: keep the inserted holiday text in Title 5
retain only a concise amendment-history note
remove the Trello link
laws.chronology_conclusion: Full source recovered. The act makes a direct, determinable amendment to 5 U.S.C. 6103(a) by adding National Fast Food Workers Day. The current XML already contains the inserted holiday text.

current_xml_comparison.detail: The 5 U.S.C. 6103(a) holiday text is already present in current XML; the note is history only.

source_evidence: Full source recovered. The act makes a direct, determinable amendment to 5 U.S.C. 6103(a) by adding National Fast Food Workers Day. The current XML already contains the inserted holiday text.
laws.final_recommended_action: keep the inserted holiday text in Title 5 | retain only a concise amendment-history note | remove the Trello link
laws.exact_change: Insert National Fast Food Workers Day, June 7, after the Memorial Day item in 5 U.S.C. 6103(a).

laws.title: Public Law 27-196 | National Commission on Judicial Activity, Efficiency and Accountability Act
laws.status_basis: Full source recovered. This is a temporary commission with fixed reporting deadlines and a self-termination clause; by the current date the commission has expired by its own terms, and the current XML treats it as history only.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date, and definitions.
provisions.evidence: law.txt SECTION I provides the act name and basic governing clauses.

provisions.text_summary: States the commission's purposes.
provisions.evidence: law.txt SECTION II defines the commission's mission and scope.

provisions.text_summary: Sets the commission's functions and investigation duties.
provisions.evidence: law.txt SECTION III authorizes investigations and recommendations about the judiciary and the Public Defender's Office.

provisions.text_summary: Defines membership, qualifications, appointments, meeting rules, quorum, and vacancies.
provisions.evidence: law.txt SECTION IV sets the commission's structure and membership rules.

provisions.text_summary: Gives the commission powers, including hearings and evidence gathering.
provisions.evidence: law.txt SECTION V confers hearings and evidence powers on the commission.

provisions.text_summary: Requires interim and final reports, then terminates the commission and transfers records to NARA.
provisions.evidence: law.txt SECTION VI sets report deadlines and SECTION VII terminates the commission after the final report period.

current_implementation.detail: Current XML keeps the act as a history note; the commission self-terminated by its own terms.

recommended_actions: keep the act as history only
do not add permanent Code text
remove the Trello link

laws.title: Public Law 28-201 | RESET Act
laws.status_basis: The act is a live repealer with a savings clause and no self-repeal or sunset. It remains current law as statutory history at the closest subject-matter anchor.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the Removing Excess Statutes and Extraneous Texts Act, or RESET Act.

provisions.text_summary: Purpose to clean up the public-law database.
provisions.evidence: To cleanup the Public Law Database and simplify our Public Law Database.

provisions.text_summary: Savings clause preserving prior repeals.
provisions.evidence: Any Public Law that is repealed by one of the laws being repealed in subsection (b) shall remain repealed.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals the listed public laws in full.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal each listed public law and leave any already-repealed law in its repealed state.
provisions.evidence: The following laws shall be repealed.

provisions.text_summary: Severability.
provisions.evidence: If any provision of this Act is held invalid, the remainder of the Act shall not be affected.

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective date.
provisions.evidence: This Act shall become effective upon enactment.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML keeps a concise historical note at the Title 2 anchor; no permanent Code text is required.

recommended_actions: Retain a concise statutory history note.
Record the listed repeals at the affected laws where they are not already tracked.
Remove the Trello link.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The act is a live repealer with a savings clause and no self-repeal or sunset. It remains current law as statutory history at the closest subject-matter anchor.

current_xml_comparison.detail: The current XML keeps a concise historical note at the Title 2 anchor; no permanent Code text is required.

source_evidence: The act is a live repealer with a savings clause and no self-repeal or sunset. It remains current law as statutory history at the closest subject-matter anchor.
laws.final_recommended_action: Retain a concise statutory history note. | Record the listed repeals at the affected laws where they are not already tracked. | Remove the Trello link.
laws.exact_change: Repeal each listed public law and leave any already-repealed law in its repealed state.
